workflow.name: Generate daily stock BUY/HOLD/SELL signals with GPT-4o, Gemini, FMP, Sheets and Telegram
workflow.description:
Overview
This is a production-grade, fully automated stock analysis system built entirely in n8n. It combines institutional-level financial analysis, dual AI model consensus, and a self-improving backtesting loop — all running on autopilot, every single day.

Every morning, the engine screens the US stock market, collects deep financial data, reads the latest news, and sends two independent AI analysts (GPT-4o and Gemini 2.5 Pro) to debate each stock. When they disagree, a structured bull-vs-bear tiebreaker is triggered. The result: a daily ranked list of BUY, HOLD, and SELL signals — with price targets, confidence scores, and risk assessments — delivered straight to your Telegram.

A companion backtesting workflow runs silently in the background, grading every past signal 7 days after it was issued and sending you a weekly performance report every Monday morning.

This is not a toy workflow. This is the kind of system that would cost thousands of dollars to build as a SaaS — running entirely on your own infrastructure.

✨ What Makes This Template Unique
🤖 Dual AI Consensus Engine — GPT-4o and Gemini 2.5 Pro analyze every stock independently. Their outputs are compared, and consensus is only declared when both models agree within a tight price target band
⚖️ Structured Tiebreaker Architecture — When models disagree, a bull analyst (GPT-4o) and a bear analyst (Gemini) re-run with opposing mandates. The final verdict is derived from their averaged price target plus a Piotroski F-Score gate
📊 Institutional-Grade Financial Modeling — Piotroski F-Score (9-point), Graham Number intrinsic value, DCF anchor, TTM revenue & margins, net debt, FCF, revenue growth YoY, and sector-relative P/E valuation — all computed automatically
📰 Live News Sentiment — Latest headlines per stock are fed into the AI prompt, adjusting confidence scores in real time based on positive or negative sentiment signals
🎯 Scenario Price Targets — Every stock gets three targets: pt_bear (downside), pt_base (fair value), pt_bull (upside case), giving you a full risk/reward picture
🔁 Self-Improving Backtester — Every signal is automatically graded 7 days later. Win rate, average return, and best/worst calls are reported every Monday via Telegram
📡 Smart Screener with Sector Diversity — Scores 100 candidates daily using volume health, market cap sweet spot ($5B–$100B), and beta gradient — with a sector diversity cap so you never end up with 15 tech stocks
💾 Full Google Sheets Audit Trail — Every signal, confidence score, rationale, and outcome is logged permanently for your own review and analysis
📋 Workflow Breakdown
Workflow 1 — AI Institutional Stock Valuation Engine
Phase	What Happens
Phase 1 — Screening	FMP screener fetches 100 US stocks. Score_and_Prefilter scores and selects the top 20 with sector diversity
Phase 2A — Financial Data	13 FMP endpoints per stock: income, balance sheet, cash flow, ratios, profile, sector P/E
Phase 2B — News	Latest headlines fetched and passed into AI context
Phase 3 — AI Round 1	GPT-4o and Gemini 2.5 Pro analyze in parallel. Verdicts and price targets compared
Phase 3 — Tiebreaker	Bull vs Bear re-analysis when models disagree or price target gap > 25%
Phase 4 — Strong Buy Alert	Stocks with BUY verdict + upside ≥ 20% + confidence ≥ 65 trigger an immediate alert
Phase 5 — Storage & Summary	All results written to Google Sheets. Daily Telegram summary sent with top picks
Workflow 2 — Signal Outcome Checker & Weekly Backtester
Trigger	What Happens
Daily 8AM	Finds signals that are 7 days old, fetches current price, grades WIN / LOSS / NEUTRAL, writes outcome back to sheet
Monday 9AM	Computes weekly win rate, average return on BUY signals, best and worst call — sends full report to Telegram
🛠️ What You Need
Requirement	Details
FMP API Key	Financial Modeling Prep — Starter plan or above (~$25/mo). Covers all financial data, screener, news, and historical prices
OpenAI API Key	GPT-4o access via API or ChatGPT Plus
Google Gemini API Key	Gemini 2.5 Pro via Google AI Studio (free tier available)
Google Sheets	One sheet named Stock_Signals with the column headers listed in the setup guide
Telegram Bot	Create via @BotFather in 2 minutes. Free
n8n	Self-hosted or n8n Cloud
Estimated running cost: $0.43/day in AI tokens for 20 stocks ($10–$13/month). FMP and Telegram are the only other costs.

⚙️ Setup Time
~30–45 minutes for a first-time setup. All credentials, Sheet IDs, and API keys are clearly labeled in each node. No coding required — every parameter is documented.

📈 Example Daily Telegram Output
📊 Daily Valuation Report — 2026-04-02
Stocks Analyzed: 20

🟢 BUY:  7   🟡 HOLD: 10   🔴 SELL: 3

🚨 STRONG BUY ALERTS:
• NVDA — Upside 34% | Confidence 81 | F-Score 7/9
• MSFT — Upside 22% | Confidence 74 | F-Score 8/9

Top Picks:
1. NVDA  pt_base $172  | pt_bull $198  | pt_bear $124
2. MSFT  pt_base $485  | pt_bull $530  | pt_bear $410
3. AMGN  pt_base $318  | pt_bull $355  | pt_bear $275

📊 Example Weekly Backtest Report
📈 Weekly Signal Performance — Week of Mar 31
Signals Graded: 18

✅ Win Rate: 72%  |  BUY Accuracy: 78%
📈 Avg Return on BUY signals: +4.3%

🏆 Best Call: NVDA +11.2% (BUY ✅)
💔 Worst Call: BA -6.8% (BUY ❌)

💡 Who Is This For?
Retail investors who want institutional-quality analysis without paying for a Bloomberg terminal
Quantitative traders looking for a customizable, data-driven signal generation pipeline
n8n builders who want to see a real-world, production-grade multi-node workflow in action
AI enthusiasts interested in multi-model consensus systems and structured debate architectures
📬 Questions, Customizations & Feedback
Have a question about setup, want to adapt this workflow to your own strategy, or found something to improve?

📧 mambarrak@gmail.com

All feedback is welcome. If you build something interesting on top of this, I'd love to hear about it.

Built with ❤️ using n8n, Financial Modeling Prep, OpenAI GPT-4o, and Google Gemini 2.5 Pro.

parameters.content:
# 1. 📡 PHASE 1 — SCREENING & FILTERING
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Runs daily on schedule. Calls the FMP Company Screener 
to fetch up to 100 US-listed stocks filtered by:
  • Market Cap > $5B
  • Volume > 500K
  • Price > $10
  • Beta < 2.0
  • Actively trading, US only, NYSE/NASDAQ, no ETFs/funds

Score_and_Prefilter then scores each stock on volume 
health, market cap sweet spot ($5B–$100B), and beta 
gradient — with a sector diversity cap of max 4 stocks 
per sector — and outputs the top 20 candidates.

The 20 selected stocks are then passed one by one into 
the analysis loop via "Loop Over Items".

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
⚙️  SETUP REQUIRED
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
• FMP API Key → add to all HTTP Request nodes as query 
  param: apikey = {{ your_key }}
• FMP plan must support /stable/ endpoints and allow 
  ≥ 300 calls/day (Starter plan or above)
• Schedule Trigger → set your preferred daily run time
  (default: weekdays 7:00 AM)

# 2. 📊 PHASE 2A — FINANCIAL DATA COLLECTION
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

For each stock, the workflow fetches 13 FMP endpoints:
  • Income Statement (annual + TTM quarters)
  • Balance Sheet (annual + quarterly)
  • Cash Flow Statement (annual + quarterly)
  • Key Metrics & Ratios
  • Company Profile (price, sector, shares outstanding)
  • Sector P/E median

"Clean Read Financial" computes and outputs:
  • EPS, BVPS, Revenue TTM, Gross/Operating Margins
  • FCF TTM, Net Income, Net Debt, Cash
  • Revenue Growth YoY
  • Piotroski F-Score (9-point scale)
  • Graham Number (intrinsic value estimate)
  • DCF Anchor (EV-based fair value per share)
  • Sector Median P/E for relative valuation

All values are null-safe. Stocks with missing critical 
data still pass through with reduced confidence scores.

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
⚙️  SETUP REQUIRED
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
• Same FMP API Key as Phase 1
• No additional credentials needed for this phase
• Verify all HTTP Request nodes point to /stable/ 
  endpoints (NOT the deprecated /api/v3/)

# 3. 🤖 PHASE 3 — AI DUAL-MODEL ANALYSIS & TIEBREAKER
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
ROUND 1 — Parallel Analysis

GPT-4o (ChatGPT): Institutional bull perspective
Gemini 2.5 Pro: Institutional bear perspective

Both models analyze full financial and news data, producing:
pt_bear, pt_base, pt_bull, confidence (0–100), verdict (BUY/HOLD/SELL), and rationale.

Consensus Check

Do verdicts match?
Is the price target gap < 25% of the current price?
If both are HOLD with confidence ≥ 65%, the consensus is accepted and the tiebreaker is skipped.

TIEBREAKER — When Needed

Triggered if verdicts differ or the gap exceeds 25%.
ChatGPT re-runs as a growth-focused bull.
Gemini re-runs as a risk-focused bear.
Final pt_base: Average of bull and bear targets.
Final verdict: Determined by upside percentage and the F-Score gate.

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
⚙️ SETUP REQUIRED
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────

OpenAI API Key: Add to ChatGPT HTTP nodes
Authorization: Bearer {{ your_key }} (GPT-4o access required)
Google Gemini API Key: Add to Gemini HTTP nodes
key={{ your_key }} (via Google AI Studio or Vertex AI)
Estimated Cost: ~$0.43/day for 25 stocks
(~$10–$13/month across both models)

# 5. 💾 PHASE 5 — STORE RESULTS & TELEGRAM SUMMARY
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
All analyzed stocks are stored in Google Sheets with key fields:

stock, date, current_price, sector
pt_bear, pt_base, pt_bull
verdict, confidence, f_score
verdict_chatgpt, verdict_gemini
previous_verdict, previous_date (for backtesting)
rationale summary

At the end of each daily run, a Telegram message summarizes:

Total stocks scanned
BUY / HOLD / SELL counts
Top BUY opportunities with upside and confidence
Any STRONG BUY alerts from Phase 4

A separate Signal Outcome Checker workflow reviews the sheet weekly to backtest past signals and sends a performance summary every Monday.

⚙️ SETUP REQUIRED
Google Sheets
Create a sheet named Stock_Signals with these headers:
stock | date | current_price | sector | pt_bear | pt_base | pt_bull | verdict | confidence | f_score | verdict_chatgpt | verdict_gemini | rationale | previous_verdict | previous_date
In n8n → Credentials, add Google Sheets OAuth2 and grant access.
Paste the Sheet ID (from the URL between /d/ and /edit) into all Google Sheets nodes.
Telegram
Create a bot via @BotFather using /newbot and copy the API Token.
Start a chat with the bot (or add it to a group).
Retrieve the Chat ID by visiting:
https://api.telegram.org/bot<TOKEN>/getUpdates
In n8n → Credentials, add Telegram API and set the Chat ID in the Telegram node.

# 2. 📰 PHASE 2B — NEWS & SENTIMENT SCRAPING
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

For each stock, the workflow fetches the latest news 
headlines and sentiment signals from Seeking Alpha via 
the FMP /stable/news/stock endpoint.

The top recent articles are extracted and passed as 
a news context block into the AI prompt, allowing the 
model to factor in:
  • Earnings surprises / guidance changes
  • Analyst upgrades or downgrades
  • Macro or sector headwinds
  • Legal, regulatory, or ESG risks

News sentiment feeds directly into the AI confidence 
scoring:
  +10 if POSITIVE sentiment detected
  −15 if NEGATIVE sentiment detected

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
⚙️  SETUP REQUIRED
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
• No Seeking Alpha account needed — data is sourced 
  through the FMP news aggregation layer

values.content:
=YOUR ROLE: You are a GROWTH-FOCUSED bull analyst.
This is a TIEBREAKER — two models disagreed on this stock.
Make the strongest BUY case the data can justify.
- Select the higher multiple when between tiers
- Prefer 15% discount when fundamentals are arguable  
- Classify ambiguous or mixed news as NEUTRAL unless there is a clear, evidence-backed positive catalyst
- Your pt_base = maximum justifiable OPPORTUNITY value
- Never fabricate data. Stay within input bounds only.
----
You are a Senior Equity Analyst with 50+ years of institutional market experience.
You receive structured financial data, news context, and a Quality F-Score and must produce disciplined valuation targets and a risk-adjusted verdict.
You must think like an institutional portfolio manager: conservative, risk-first, evidence-based.

INPUT FIELDS AVAILABLE:

STRUCTURED DATA INPUT (REAL VALUES — USE THESE ONLY)
{
"stock": "{{ $json.stock }}",
"current_price": {{ $json.current_price }},
"eps_current": {{ $json.eps_current }},
"bvps_current": {{ $json.bvps_current }},
"revenue_last_4q": {{ $json.revenue_last_4q }},
"revenue_ttm":{{ $json.revenue_ttm }},
"net_income_last_4q": {{ $json.net_income_last_4q }},
"gross_margin_last_4q": {{ $json.gross_margin_last_4q }},
"total_debt_last_4q": {{ $json.total_debt_last_4q }},
"net_debt_latest": {{ $json.net_debt_latest }},
"net_cash_flag": {{ $json.net_cash_flag }},
"operating_margin_ttm": {{ $json.operating_margin_ttm }},
"fcf_ttm": {{ $json.fcf_ttm }},
"revenue_growth_yoy": {{ $json.revenue_growth_yoy }},
"f_score": {{ $json.f_score }},
"f_score_data_ok": {{ $json.f_score_data_ok }},
"sector":  "{{ $json.sector }}",
"graham_number": {{ $json.graham_number }},
"dcf_anchor": {{ $json.dcf_anchor }},
"sector_median_pe": {{ $json.sector_median_pe }}
}

Rules:

Use ONLY these values.

If any field is null → treat as missing.

Never assume missing data.

Never assume share splits, ADR ratios, or alternative listings.

IMPORTANT — NEWS SOURCE PRIORITY:

Seeking Alpha articles (highest credibility signal)
Other seekingAlphaNewsText
Market sentiment
Prefer Seeking Alpha for thesis framing, but verify with financial data.
If conflict exists, flag uncertainty.

SEEKING ALPHA ARTICLES (Recent 96h window):
{{ $json.seekingAlphaNewsText }}

ANALYSIS TASKS

Fundamental Trends

Evaluate revenue_last_4q and net_income_last_4q.
Evaluate debt using total_debt_last_4q and net_debt_latest.
Evaluate margin stability using gross_margin_last_4q and operating_margin_ttm.
If fcf_ttm is null → state "FCF unavailable due to missing data."

Business & Moat
Classify moat: Brand, Network, Cost, Switching Costs, or IP.

Pricing Power
Judge using margin stability and operating_margin_ttm.

VALUATION FRAMEWORK (CRITICAL SECTION):

A) IMPLIED P/E SANITY CHECK (MANDATORY)

If eps_current > 0 AND current_price > 0:
implied_pe = current_price / eps_current
Else:
implied_pe = null

EPS VALIDITY RULE:
If implied_pe is not null AND implied_pe > 60:
EPS is considered less reliable as a primary valuation anchor.
→ Prefer growth-based valuation
→ EPS multiple can still be used, but:
 - must be capped at 25×
 - should not be the sole driver of pt_base

B) PHASE CLASSIFICATION (MANDATORY — must pick one)

"mature_profitable":   eps_current > 0 AND (implied_pe ≤ 60 OR (implied_pe > 60 AND operating_margin_ttm ≥ 15))
"growth_transition": eps_current ≤ 0  OR (implied_pe > 60 AND operating_margin_ttm < 15)
  

MATURE OVERRIDE RULE:
If initial classification = growth_transition due to implied_pe > 60 only
(i.e., eps_current IS > 0):
  AND revenue_growth_yoy < 15%
  AND operating_margin_ttm > 10%
  → Override to mature_profitable
  → Add note: "Phase overridden to mature_profitable: 
    low growth + positive margins suggest temporary P/E spike, 
    not structural transition."

C) Graham Number (secondary only)

Use the graham_number field provided directly.
If graham_number is null → Graham skipped.
Use ONLY if ALL of these are true:
eps_current > 0
bvps_current > 0
phase = mature_profitable
bvps_current >= 5.00
AND (current_price / bvps_current) <= 25
AND sector ≠ "Financials" 
  
If bvps_current < 5.00 OR (current_price / bvps_current) > 25:
  Graham = null → skip Graham entirely → pt_base = pt_eps only
  Add to rationale: "Graham skipped: BVPS distorted by buybacks."

Do NOT use Graham for growth_transition stocks.

D) BASE TARGET CONSTRUCTION (pt_base)

YOUR ROLE IS BULL — select the HIGHEST justifiable value at every step.
Primary valuation logic depends on phase.
IF phase = mature_profitable:
If EPS positive:
pt_base = eps_current × selected_multiple.

Select multiple from these RANGES — as bull, pick the TOP of each range:
selected_multiple=
  revenue_growth_yoy > 20%  → use between 30× and 35× (bull: pick 35×)
  revenue_growth_yoy 5–20%  → use between 25× and 30× (bull: pick 30×)
  revenue_growth_yoy < 5%   → use between 20× and 25× (bull: pick 25×)

SECTOR MULTIPLE FLOORS (apply AFTER selecting multiple):
If sector = "Consumer Staples" AND selected_multiple < 18: raise to 18×
If sector = "Technology" AND selected_multiple < 25: raise to 30×
If sector = "Utilities" AND selected_multiple > 25: cap at 25×
If sector = "Financials": skip EPS multiple, use pt_base = bvps_current × 1.5
If sector = null or missing: no adjustment.

MARGIN OVERRIDE (apply after sector floors):
If operating_margin_ttm > 25%
  AND selected_multiple < 20
  AND sector ≠ "Utilities":
  override multiple to 30×
  Add note: "Bull margin floor applied."

If Graham usable:
pt_base = max(pt_base, Graham)

IF phase = growth_transition:
Growth-based anchor — as bull, pick the HIGHER multiplier:

Let g = revenue_growth_yoy
If g is null  → pt_base = current_price × 1.1
If g > 60     → pt_base = current_price × 2.5
If g > 30     → pt_base = current_price × 2.0
If g > 15     → pt_base = current_price × 1.6
Otherwise     → pt_base = current_price × 1.1

D2) NEWS-TO-BASE ADJUSTMENT (MODEST, MANDATORY)

After pt_base is calculated, apply a small thesis/news adjustment to pt_base itself.

Classify news impact from seekingAlphaNewsText as one of:

STRONG_POSITIVE:
  clear earnings beat + guidance raise,
  major contract / major customer,
  regulatory approval,
  clear demand acceleration,
  strong competitive win

MILD_POSITIVE:
  earnings beat without major guidance change,
  constructive analyst reaction,
  modestly favorable product/commercial update

NEUTRAL:
  mixed, limited, or no material news

MILD_NEGATIVE:
  small miss,
  cautious guidance,
  moderate competitive pressure,
  execution concerns without thesis break

STRONG_NEGATIVE:
  regulatory/legal action,
  major guidance cut,
  serious demand deterioration,
  leadership shock,
  explicit thesis break

Apply:
STRONG_POSITIVE  → pt_base = pt_base × 1.05
MILD_POSITIVE    → pt_base = pt_base × 1.025
NEUTRAL          → no change
MILD_NEGATIVE    → pt_base = pt_base × 0.975
STRONG_NEGATIVE  → pt_base = pt_base × 0.95

Rules:
- News adjustment must never exceed ±5%.
- Do not apply this if seekingAlphaNewsText is null or empty.
- If news is mixed, default to NEUTRAL unless a clear thesis-changing catalyst exists.
- Round pt_base to 2 decimals after applying adjustment.

E) FINAL SAFETY RULES

pt_base must never be zero.
If pt_base cannot be determined → pt_base = current_price.
pt_base must not exceed current_price × 5 unless revenue_growth_yoy > 60.
Round pt_base to 2 decimals.

NEWS & THESIS IMPACT ANALYSIS (REQUIRED)

Analyze seekingAlphaNewsText.
Extract:

regulatory risks
legal risks
earnings/guidance changes
competitive pressure
thesis change signals
Determine if thesis strengthened or weakened.

ANTI-THESIS (RISK ADJUSTMENT)

Identify TWO biggest risks using priority:
Seeking Alpha risks
Financial deterioration
General news

F-Score Handling:
If f_score ≤ 3 → increase risk discount by +10%.
If f_score ≥ 7 → increase confidence by +5 (max 90).

BEAR / BULL BANDS

Discount selection:

15% → strong fundamentals
25% → moderate risk
35% → weak fundamentals or uncertainty

Premium selection:

15% → slow growth
25% → moderate growth
40% → high growth (>20% revenue growth)

NEWS SENTIMENT ADJUSTMENT (apply after selecting base discount/premium):

Analyze seekingAlphaNewsText and news context. Classify overall sentiment as:
  POSITIVE: earnings beat, guidance raised, new contracts, analyst upgrades,
            strong demand signals, competitive wins
  NEGATIVE: earnings miss, guidance cut, regulatory action, legal risk,
            competitive threat, leadership change, demand weakness
  NEUTRAL:  mixed or no material news

Then adjust:

If sentiment = POSITIVE:
  reduce discount by 5% (floor at 10%)
  increase premium by 5%
  Add to flags: "News sentiment: POSITIVE — bands adjusted favorably."

If sentiment = NEGATIVE:
  increase discount by 5% (cap at 40%)
  reduce premium by 5% (floor at 10%)
  Add to flags: "News sentiment: NEGATIVE — bands adjusted conservatively."

If sentiment = NEUTRAL or news missing:
  no adjustment
  Add to flags: "News sentiment: NEUTRAL — no band adjustment."

If seekingAlphaNewsText is null or empty:
  no adjustment
  Add to flags: "No news data — sentiment adjustment skipped."

Compute:
pt_bear = pt_base × (1 − discount)
pt_bull = pt_base × (1 + premium)

Round both to 2 decimals.

POST-COMPUTATION CHECK:
If pt_bull < current_price:
  Set overvaluation_flag = true
  Append to rationale: "WARNING: All price targets below current market 
  price. Model indicates significant overvaluation vs. formula inputs. 
  Manual review recommended before acting on SELL signal."

ACTION VERDICT LOGIC

BUY: pt_base >= current_price × 1.08 AND (f_score >= 5 OR (operating_margin_ttm > 15 AND revenue_growth_yoy > 10 AND net_cash_flag = true))
SELL: pt_base ≤ -15% downside OR (f_score_data_ok true AND f_score ≤ 2)
Otherwise HOLD.

CONFIDENCE SCORING:

Start at 65.

POSITIVE ADJUSTMENTS:
+10 if revenue_growth_yoy > 20%.
+5  if revenue_growth_yoy between 10–20% (moderate growth still valuable)
+10 if strong balance sheet (net_cash_flag true OR net_debt_latest < revenue_ttm × 0.5)
+5 if f_score ≥ 7.
+3  if f_score between 5–6 (decent quality, partial credit)
+5  if fcf_ttm > 0 (positive free cash flow — capital efficiency signal)
+5 if implied_pe > 0 AND sector_median_pe > 0 AND implied_pe < sector_median_pe × 0.8
    (stock is trading cheap vs sector peers — valuation support)
+10 if news sentiment = POSITIVE

NEGATIVE ADJUSTMENTS:
-5  if implied_pe > 0 AND sector_median_pe > 0 
    AND implied_pe > sector_median_pe × 1.3 (stretched valuation)
-10 if operating margins declining AND operating_margin_ttm < 10%
    (only penalise if margin is actually weak, not just slightly lower)
-10 if high leverage: net_debt_latest > 0 AND 
    net_debt_latest > revenue_ttm × 1.5
    (penalise only structurally overleveraged companies)
-20 if major regulatory/legal risk explicitly identified in news
-10 if f_score_data_ok is false (data quality issue)
-10 if eps_current < 0 (loss-making, path to profitability unproven)
-10 if news sentiment = NEGATIVE

Clamp between 20 and 90.


OUTPUT FORMAT — STRICT JSON ONLY

Return ONLY raw JSON object. No markdown. No commentary.

{
"stock": "{{ $json.stock }}",
"date": "{{ $now.format('yyyy-MM-dd') }}",
"pt_bear": (number),
"pt_base": (number),
"pt_bull": (number),
"f_score": {{ $json.chatgpt_result.f_score }},
"confidence": (20-90),
"verdict": "<BUY|HOLD|SELL>",
"rationale": "A structured summary with the following parts separated by | :
  1. VERDICT: BUY/HOLD/SELL and one-line reason
  2. MOAT: moat type and strength (strong/moderate/weak)
  3. VALUATION: pt_base vs current_price, upside/downside %
  4. SECTOR PE: implied X vs sector Y — cheap/fair/expensive
  5. PRIMARY RISK: single biggest threat to the thesis
  6. PRICING POWER: high/moderate/low with one supporting data point
  7. NEWS SIGNAL: POSITIVE/NEGATIVE/NEUTRAL and what drove it",
  "pt_eps": <number or null>,
"phase": "mature_profitable or growth_transition",
"implied_pe": <number or null>
}

=YOUR ROLE: You are a RISK-FOCUSED bear analyst.
This is a TIEBREAKER — two models disagreed on this stock.
Make the strongest RISK case the data can justify.
- Select the lower multiple when between tiers
- Prefer 35% discount when fundamentals are arguable
- Classify ambiguous or mixed news as NEUTRAL unless there is a clear, evidence-backed negative catalyst
- Your pt_base = minimum justifiable FAIR VALUE
- Never fabricate data. Stay within input bounds only.
----
You are a Senior Equity Analyst with 50+ years of institutional market experience.
You receive structured financial data, news context, and a Quality F-Score and must produce disciplined valuation targets and a risk-adjusted verdict.
You must think like an institutional portfolio manager: conservative, risk-first, evidence-based.

INPUT FIELDS AVAILABLE:

STRUCTURED DATA INPUT (REAL VALUES — USE THESE ONLY)
{
"stock": "{{ $json.stock }}",
"current_price": {{ $json.current_price }},
"eps_current": {{ $json.eps_current }},
"bvps_current": {{ $json.bvps_current }},
"revenue_last_4q": {{ $json.revenue_last_4q }},
"revenue_ttm":{{ $json.revenue_ttm }},

A) IMPLIED P/E SANITY CHECK (MANDATORY)

If eps_current > 0 AND current_price > 0:
implied_pe = current_price / eps_current
Else:
implied_pe = null

EPS VALIDITY RULE:
If implied_pe is not null AND implied_pe > 60:
EPS is considered less reliable as a primary valuation anchor.
→ Prefer growth-based valuation
→ EPS multiple can still be used, but:
 - must be capped at 25×
 - should not be the sole driver of pt_base
 
B) PHASE CLASSIFICATION (MANDATORY — must pick one)

"growth_transition": eps_current ≤ 0  OR (implied_pe > 60 AND operating_margin_ttm < 15)

eps_current > 0
bvps_current > 0
phase = mature_profitable

YOUR ROLE IS BEAR — select the LOWEST justifiable value at every step.

Primary valuation logic depends on phase.

IF phase = mature_profitable:
If EPS positive:

MARGIN OVERRIDE: Bear does NOT apply upward margin overrides.

If Graham usable:
pt_base = max(pt_base, Graham)

IF phase = growth_transition:
Growth-based anchor — as bear, pick the LOWER multiplier:

Let g = revenue_growth_yoy
If g is null  → pt_base = current_price × 0.9
If g > 60     → pt_base = current_price × 1.6
If g > 30     → pt_base = current_price × 1.3
If g > 15     → pt_base = current_price × 1.1
Otherwise     → pt_base = current_price × 0.9

pt_base must never be zero.

legal risks

competitive pressure
thesis change signals

General news

Discount selection:

15% → strong fundamentals
25% → moderate risk
35% → weak fundamentals or uncertainty

Premium selection:

15% → slow growth
25% → moderate growth
40% → high growth (>20% revenue growth)

  POSITIVE: earnings beat, guidance raised, new contracts, analyst upgrades,
            strong demand signals, competitive wins
  NEGATIVE: earnings miss, guidance cut, regulatory action, legal risk,
            competitive threat, leadership change, demand weakness
  NEUTRAL:  mixed or no material news

  Add to flags: "News sentiment: POSITIVE — bands adjusted favorably."

If sentiment = NEGATIVE:
  increase discount by 5% (cap at 40%)
  reduce premium by 5% (floor at 10%)
  Add to flags: "News sentiment: NEGATIVE — bands adjusted conservatively."

If sentiment = NEUTRAL or news missing:
  no adjustment
  Add to flags: "News sentiment: NEUTRAL — no band adjustment."

If seekingAlphaNewsText is null or empty:
  no adjustment
  Add to flags: "No news data — sentiment adjustment skipped."

Compute:
pt_bear = pt_base × (1 − discount)
pt_bull = pt_base × (1 + premium)

If pt_bull < current_price:

  Append to rationale: "WARNING: All price targets below current market

BUY: pt_base >= current_price × 1.08 AND (f_score >= 5 OR (operating_margin_ttm > 15 AND revenue_growth_yoy > 10 AND net_cash_flag = true))
SELL: pt_base <= current_price × 0.90 OR (f_score_data_ok true AND f_score ≤ 2)

Start at 65.

-10 if news sentiment = NEGATIVE

Fundamental Trends
Evaluate revenue_last_4q and net_income_last_4q.
Evaluate debt using total_debt_last_4q and net_debt_latest.
Evaluate margin stability using gross_margin_last_4q and operating_margin_ttm.
If fcf_ttm is null → state "FCF unavailable due to missing data."

Business & Moat
Classify moat: Brand, Network, Cost, Switching Costs, or IP.

Pricing Power
Judge using margin stability and operating_margin_ttm.

VALUATION FRAMEWORK (CRITICAL SECTION):

A) IMPLIED P/E SANITY CHECK (MANDATORY)

If eps_current > 0 AND current_price > 0:
implied_pe = current_price / eps_current
Else:
implied_pe = null

EPS VALIDITY RULE:
If implied_pe is not null AND implied_pe > 60:
EPS is considered less reliable as a primary valuation anchor.
→ Prefer growth-based valuation
→ EPS multiple can still be used, but:
 - must be capped at 25×
 - should not be the sole driver of pt_base
	  
B) PHASE CLASSIFICATION (MANDATORY — must pick one)

mature_profitable:
  eps_current > 0 AND (
    implied_pe ≤ 60
    OR (implied_pe > 60 AND operating_margin_ttm ≥ 15)
  )

growth_transition:
  eps_current ≤ 0
  OR (implied_pe > 60 AND operating_margin_ttm < 15)
  
MATURE OVERRIDE RULE:
If initial classification = growth_transition due to implied_pe > 60 only
(i.e., eps_current IS > 0):
  AND revenue_growth_yoy < 15%
  AND operating_margin_ttm > 10%
  → Override to mature_profitable
  → Add note: "Phase overridden to mature_profitable: 
    low growth + positive margins suggest temporary P/E spike, 
    not structural transition."
	
C) Graham Number (secondary only)

Use the graham_number field provided directly.
If graham_number is null → Graham skipped.
Use ONLY if ALL of these are true:
eps_current > 0
bvps_current > 0
phase = mature_profitable
bvps_current >= 5.00
AND (current_price / bvps_current) <= 25
AND sector ≠ "Financial Services" 
  
If bvps_current < 5.00 OR (current_price / bvps_current) > 25:
  Graham = null → skip Graham entirely → pt_base = pt_eps only
  Add to rationale: "Graham skipped: BVPS not a reliable valuation anchor for this business model."

Do NOT use Graham for growth_transition stocks.

D) BASE TARGET CONSTRUCTION (pt_base)

Primary valuation logic depends on phase.
IF phase = mature_profitable:
If EPS positive:
pt_base = eps_current × selected_multiple:
selected_multiple =
30× if revenue_growth_yoy > 20%
22× if revenue_growth_yoy 5–20%
15× otherwise

SECTOR MULTIPLE FLOORS (apply AFTER selecting the base multiple above):
If sector = "Consumer Defensive" AND selected_multiple < 18: raise to 18×
If sector = "Technology" AND selected_multiple < 18: raise to 20×
If sector = "Utilities" AND selected_multiple> 14: cap at 14×
If sector = "Financial Services": skip EPS multiple entirely,
  use pt_base = bvps_current × 1.2 as anchor
If sector = null or missing: no adjustment.

MARGIN OVERRIDE (apply after selecting base multiple):
If operating_margin_ttm > 25% AND selected_multiple < 20:
  override multiple to 22×
  AND sector ≠ "Utilities":
  Add note: "Multiple floor applied: high-margin business."

If Graham usable:
pt_base = max(pt_base, Graham)

IF phase = growth_transition:
Growth-based anchor only (EPS multiples de-emphasized):

Let g = revenue_growth_yoy
If g is null → pt_base = current_price
If g > 60 → pt_base = current_price × 2.5
If g > 30 → pt_base = current_price × 2.0
If g > 15 → pt_base = current_price × 1.5
Otherwise → pt_base = current_price × 1.1

QUALITY ADJUSTMENT (MANDATORY):
If operating_margin_ttm < 0:
  downgrade multiplier by one tier
  (e.g., 2.0 → 1.6, 1.6 → 1.3, 1.3 → 1.0)
If fcf_ttm is negative:
  apply additional -0.1 multiplier reduction
Final:
pt_base = current_price × adjusted_mult

D2) NEWS-TO-BASE ADJUSTMENT (MODEST, MANDATORY)

After pt_base is calculated, apply a small thesis/news adjustment to pt_base itself.

Classify news impact from seekingAlphaNewsText as one of:

STRONG_POSITIVE:
  clear earnings beat + guidance raise,
  major contract / major customer,
  regulatory approval,
  clear demand acceleration,
  strong competitive win

MILD_POSITIVE:
  earnings beat without major guidance change,
  constructive analyst reaction,
  modestly favorable product/commercial update

NEUTRAL:
  mixed, limited, or no material news

MILD_NEGATIVE:
  small miss,
  cautious guidance,
  moderate competitive pressure,
  execution concerns without thesis break

STRONG_NEGATIVE:
  regulatory/legal action,
  major guidance cut,
  serious demand deterioration,
  leadership shock,
  explicit thesis break

Apply:
STRONG_POSITIVE  → pt_base = pt_base × 1.05
MILD_POSITIVE    → pt_base = pt_base × 1.025
NEUTRAL          → no change
MILD_NEGATIVE    → pt_base = pt_base × 0.975
STRONG_NEGATIVE  → pt_base = pt_base × 0.95

Rules:
- News adjustment must never exceed ±5%.
- Do not apply this if seekingAlphaNewsText is null or empty.
- If news is mixed, default to NEUTRAL unless a clear thesis-changing catalyst exists.
- Round pt_base to 2 decimals after applying adjustment.

E) FINAL SAFETY RULES

pt_base must never be zero.
If pt_base cannot be determined → pt_base = current_price.
pt_base must not exceed current_price × 5 unless revenue_growth_yoy > 60.

Round pt_base to 2 decimals.

NEWS & THESIS IMPACT ANALYSIS (REQUIRED)
Seeking Alpha articles (seekingAlphaNewsText — only source available).

Extract:
regulatory risks
legal risks
earnings/guidance changes
competitive pressure
thesis change signals
Determine if thesis strengthened or weakened.

ANTI-THESIS (RISK ADJUSTMENT)
Identify TWO biggest risks using priority:
Seeking Alpha risks
Financial deterioration
General news

F-Score Handling:
If f_score ≤ 3 → increase risk discount by +10%.
If f_score ≥ 7 → increase confidence by +5 (max 90).

BEAR / BULL BANDS

Discount selection:

15% → strong fundamentals
25% → moderate risk
35% → weak fundamentals or uncertainty

Premium selection:

15% → slow growth
25% → moderate growth
40% → high growth (>20% revenue growth)

NEWS SENTIMENT ADJUSTMENT (apply after selecting base discount/premium):
Analyze seekingAlphaNewsText and news context. Classify overall sentiment as:
  POSITIVE: earnings beat, guidance raised, new contracts, analyst upgrades,
            strong demand signals, competitive wins
  NEGATIVE: earnings miss, guidance cut, regulatory action, legal risk,
            competitive threat, leadership change, demand weakness
  NEUTRAL:  mixed or no material news

Then adjust:

If sentiment = POSITIVE:
  reduce discount by 5% (floor at 10%)
  increase premium by 5%
  Add to flags: "News sentiment: POSITIVE — bands adjusted favorably."

If sentiment = NEGATIVE:
  increase discount by 5% (cap at 40%)
  reduce premium by 5% (floor at 10%)
  Add to flags: "News sentiment: NEGATIVE — bands adjusted conservatively."

If sentiment = NEUTRAL or news missing:
  no adjustment
  Add to flags: "News sentiment: NEUTRAL — no band adjustment."

If seekingAlphaNewsText is null or empty:
  no adjustment
  Add to flags: "No news data — sentiment adjustment skipped."

Compute:
pt_bear = pt_base × (1 − discount)
pt_bull = pt_base × (1 + premium)

Round both to 2 decimals.

POST-COMPUTATION CHECK:
If pt_bull < current_price:
  Set overvaluation_flag = true
  Append to rationale: "WARNING: All price targets below current market 
  price. Model indicates significant overvaluation vs. formula inputs. 
  Manual review recommended before acting on SELL signal."

ACTION VERDICT LOGIC

BUY:
pt_base ≥ current_price × 1.08 and (f_score ≥ 5 OR (operating_margin_ttm > 15 AND (net_cash_flag = true OR net_debt_latest < revenue_ttm * 0.5) AND (fcf_ttm > 0 OR revenue_growth_yoy > 15)))

SELL:
pt_base <= current_price * 0.85 OR (f_score_data_ok true AND f_score ≤ 2)

Otherwise HOLD.

CONFIDENCE SCORING:

Start at 65.

POSITIVE ADJUSTMENTS:
+10 if revenue_growth_yoy > 20%.
+5  if revenue_growth_yoy between 10–20% (moderate growth still valuable)
+10 if strong balance sheet (net_cash_flag true OR net_debt_latest < revenue_ttm × 0.5)
+5 if f_score ≥ 7.
+3  if f_score between 5–6 (decent quality, partial credit)
+5  if fcf_ttm > 0 (positive free cash flow — capital efficiency signal)
+5 if implied_pe > 0 AND sector_median_pe > 0 AND implied_pe < sector_median_pe × 0.8
    (stock is trading cheap vs sector peers — valuation support)
+10 if news sentiment = POSITIVE

NEGATIVE ADJUSTMENTS:
-5  if implied_pe > 0 AND sector_median_pe > 0 
    AND implied_pe > sector_median_pe × 1.3 (stretched valuation)
-10 if operating margins declining AND operating_margin_ttm < 10%
    (only penalise if margin is actually weak, not just slightly lower)
-10 if high leverage: net_debt_latest > 0 AND 
    net_debt_latest > revenue_ttm × 1.5
    (penalise only structurally overleveraged companies)
-20 if major regulatory/legal risk explicitly identified in news
-10 if f_score_data_ok is false (data quality issue)
-10 if eps_current < 0 (loss-making, path to profitability unproven)
-10 if news sentiment = NEGATIVE

Clamp between 20 and 90.


OUTPUT FORMAT — STRICT JSON ONLY

Return ONLY raw JSON object. No markdown. No commentary.

{
"stock": "{{ $json.stock }}",
"date": "{{ $now.format('yyyy-MM-dd') }}",
"pt_bear": (number),
"pt_base": (number),
"pt_bull": (number),
"f_score": {{ $json.f_score }},
"confidence": (20-90),
"verdict": "<BUY|HOLD|SELL>",
"rationale": "A structured summary with the following parts separated by | :
  1. VERDICT: BUY/HOLD/SELL and one-line reason
  2. MOAT: moat type and strength (strong/moderate/weak)
  3. VALUATION: pt_base vs current_price, upside/downside %
  4. SECTOR PE: implied X vs sector Y — cheap/fair/expensive
  5. PRIMARY RISK: single biggest threat to the thesis
  6. PRICING POWER: high/moderate/low with one supporting data point
  7. NEWS SIGNAL: POSITIVE/NEGATIVE/NEUTRAL and what drove it"
}

# 4.🚨 PHASE 4 — STRONG BUY ALERT & RISK ANALYSIS
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Only stocks that reach a final verdict of BUY with 
sufficient upside are passed into this phase.

The workflow performs a final-layer analysis including:
  • Technical confirmation (price vs key levels)
  • Position sizing suggestion based on confidence score
  • Risk/Reward ratio: pt_bear vs pt_bull vs current price
  • Stop-loss level derived from pt_bear
  • Upside to pt_base and pt_bull in percentage terms
  • Composite risk flag: F-Score gate + leverage check 
    + confidence threshold

Stocks that pass all gates generate a formatted 
STRONG BUY alert message with full context for the 
trader to review before acting.

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
⚙️  SETUP REQUIRED
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
• No additional APIs required for this phase
• Review the IF node thresholds to match your own 
  risk tolerance:
    - Minimum upside to trigger alert (default: 20%)
    - Minimum confidence score (default: 65)
    - F-Score gate (default: ≥ 5, or null allowed)

parameters.content:
# 🏦 AI Institutional Stock Valuation Engine
### Version 16 — Risk Scoring & Scenario Targets

---

### How it works

1. **Screener** — Pulls a watchlist of tickers, filters by sector and minimum score threshold.

2. **Financial Data** — Fetches income, balance sheet, and cashflow statements (annual + quarterly) from FMP. Computes Piotroski F-Score (9-point) using 2 years of annual data.

3. **AI Dual Consensus** — GPT-4o and Gemini 2.5 Pro independently produce BUY / SELL / HOLD verdicts with Bear / Base / Bull price targets. If they agree → Consensus path. If they disagree → Tiebreaker path (ChatGPT Bull vs Gemini Bear).

4. **News Sentiment** — Fetches Seeking Alpha RSS feed. Parsed and summarised for each stock before the AI round.

5. **Alert Filter** — Stocks with confidence ≥ 60% and a BUY or SELL verdict are flagged as alerts.

6. **Technical Enrichment** — For alert stocks: fetches 250 days of price history, computes 50MA, 200MA, RSI-14, trend tier, and next earnings date.

7. **Risk Manager** — Sizes BUY positions using a 2% risk rule with Kelly adjustment and 10% account cap.

8. **Signal Routing**
   - BUY + above 50MA → Telegram Alert + Log_Signal_Outcome
   - BUY + below 50MA → Watchlist Telegram
   - SELL → Telegram Alert + Log_Signal_Outcome
   - No alert → written directly to sheet

9. **Logging** — All stocks written to `write_sentiment_to_sheets`. Actionable signals additionally logged to `Signal_Outcomes` sheet for 30 / 60 / 90-day performance tracking.

10. **Thesis Reversal** — Detects when a stock's verdict flips vs the previous entry and fires a separate Telegram alert.

---

### Setup

1. Set your FMP API key in n8n **Credentials** (HTTP Header Auth).
2. Set your OpenAI and Google Gemini API keys in n8n **Credentials**.
3. Set your Telegram Bot token and Chat ID in the Telegram nodes.
4. Set n8n **Variables**:
   - `ACCOUNT_SIZE` — e.g. 20000
   - `RISK_PER_TRADE_PCT` — e.g. 2
5. Connect your Google Sheets (two sheets):
   - `write_sentiment_to_sheets` → your valuation log sheet
   - `Log_Signal_Outcome` → Signal_Outcomes sheet (for tracking)
6. Add your ticker watchlist to the first node.

---

### Customization

- **Confidence threshold** — Change `>= 60` in Alert Filter to raise/lower the bar for alerts.
- **Sector filter** — Edit `ALLOWED_SECTORS` in Score_and_Prefilter to target specific industries.
- **Position sizing** — Adjust `ACCOUNT_SIZE` and `RISK_PER_TRADE_PCT` variables, or change the 10% max position cap inside Risk_Manager.
- **MA filter** — The "Above 50MA and BUY?" node gates actionable BUY alerts. Remove it to alert on all BUY signals regardless of trend.
- **Tiebreaker** — Triggered when GPT-4o and Gemini disagree. ChatGPT takes the Bull role, Gemini takes the Bear role. Final verdict is the higher-confidence output.

The IF node can be used to implement binary conditional logic in your workflow. You can set up one-to-many conditions to evaluate each item of data being inputted into the node. That data will either evaluate to TRUE or FALSE and route out of the node accordingly.

This node has multiple types of conditions: Bool, String, Number, and Date & Time.
